Sell drugs

Steal drugs back

Infinite supply glitch.

Run over people with car

Great game.
Sneakily adding meth samples in my weed customers pockets. Top tier gaming experience
If you're a pot head like one of my friends, or a kid who wants to do illegal things without the consequences, this is the game.

One of the funniest online co-op games I’ve played lmao. 😂 It’s actually really fun because you get to see how everything works behind the scenes, and the police chases make the game more thrilling and chaotic. It’s even better with friends, especially when everything starts going wrong hahaha. 😂

However, the game can get repetitive after a while. I just hope there will be more future updates like bigger areas, different cities, gangs, increase co-op player and more content to make the gameplay feel fresh again. ദ്ദി(ᵔᗜᵔ)
Needs better updates and bug fixes, but overall its a good and fun game!

A genuinely incredible, casual "tycoon-like" simulator that stretches well above and beyond its expectations as a "Friend-slop" experience.

Don't be put off by the questionable graphics or the subject matter of the game. The graphics, especially, are clearly a weak point of the developer, but they knock it out of the park everywhere else, from mechanics to incentives to map design to game design... it's all genuinely incredible.

What shocks me is that this game is clearly aiming for the, "Just hang out with your friend group of 4 people to play this game" genre/demographic. However, the game even stands on its own as a solo play experience (though employees become much more mandatory than when playing with friends.) The game is a [i]little[/i] easy, but I honestly prefer that in a game like this.
good game but gets boring when u complete it all, needs updates
Great game with your friends or if you are feeling like a kingpin on your own. I adore the game and can't wait for more content!

This is a very fun game and Ive played a lot on my own. However, multiplayer is unplayable. You go and start a game with your friends and then when you play again, their progress is lost. Host can still make deals but the guests have to offer samples as if they were never our customers. Things in the house get removed as well. The desync is so bad its unplayable and you end up having to delete your progress. Its very frustrating. These issues began when the Benzie's and graffiti were added to the game, which was about a year ago now.. Developers have not yet fixed the desync bug, so I am giving bad review until this is fixed.
dont sell drugs in real life dont ruin your life by selling drugs ruin your life in this game very tuff

i was all for this game until they decided to make it only for F*CKING windows. i purchased it, played it, and at first loved it, all on my steam deck. i go to boot it up today and there it was BIG AF MIDDLE FINGER pointed right at my face. hopefully the devs fix it and let it be played on all platforms available, LIKE IT USED TO F*CKING BE. because i know i ain't getting my money back at this point. F*CK

Cool and very fun game but just tried it once again since release and it's still lacking a lot of content. There is no challange at all, you can just complete the game using the 4 stock weed strains. The cartel is cool but once you blow em up there is nothing to do except making more money for no reason. Worker management is still the same, which is unnecessary complicated and split, instead of tucked in one hud element. Massive coop performance issues, NCPs are missing for players but not the host, issues with items not showing at all. Almost everything is the same at is was on the release, no improvements, no content :( Good to hear that the dev team is getting expanded but it's been over a year since release and it does not look good when it comes to the official 1.0 release. I want to be wrong but I expect this game to be in development forever, never be released or release in the state it currently is. 
Also most of the mods stopped working since some update as the devs simply changed many variable names :/ I looked forward to coming back to this game but it's still pretty empty.

Let me get started and say, YES I would recommend picking up this game.... for a session that wont last longer than around 15 to 25 hours...

Going for a 100% playthrough on this game is NOT what it's built for, the game does not have near enough content to stretch itself out over to your grind to 10 million dollars in net worth. The tedium of constantly having to refill the most moronic npc workers (which are also heavily unoptimized in terms of framerate performance) gets boring very quickly, even putting a mostly semi automatic coke farm up in the manor doesn't provide you with much. You know the game about selling and making drugs, fails to make the whole journey enjoyable when it is genuinely more profitable to make a macro that spins all the 5 slot machines at the casino to push your way to the 10 million.

On top of all this, the game is quite buggy, cops turning into stretched out billboard textures because their model got sent somewhere into the negative billions through a faulty ragdoll, multiplayer just not checking who has ever joined your session, so the atm limit suddenly does not have an effect on your friend that can just re-join spam the game to whitewash at a rate not even all 4 businesses could keep up with, and the worst one of all was my friend outright getting his whole inventory wiped, 500k robbed from under our nose because the game somehow didn't remember the same steam client joining for the 30th time.

A few achievements also outright didn't work while we we're playing multiplayer, we had spray painted effectively the whole town and ended our save with 11.3 Million in net worth only to not get either achievement (Thank god for SAM)

Everything considered, pick it up in a sale, give it a singular short playthrough that maybe doesn't even see all the way to the coke era, and you will probably remember liking your time with schedule 1. For me? I don't think I will be touching this game with a 15ft pole for the next at least 10 years till some major fixes and new additions are added to the game.

[h1]An utter betrayal of promised dopamine.[/h1]

[b]The Good (Why it hooks you)[/b]
The first few hours are a masterpiece of tension. You’re huddled in a cramped, claustrophobic safehouse, meticulously weighing out single grams, micro-managing your cash, and genuinely sweating every time you see a cop. The gritty street-survival fantasy is running at 100%. It’s a phenomenal loop.

[b]The Bad (The Betrayal)[/b]
Then, you unlock more properties.
The exact millisecond you buy properties and get a high-tier distributor automated, the game completely runs out of gas. The survival mechanics evaporate, and the entire experience pivots into a mindless, idle clicker. You aren’t a kingpin; you’re just a bored data analyst sitting in an empty warehouse watching numbers go up and waiting on money-laundering timers to tick down.

The difficulty curve flatlines so hard it feels like a scam. I got so bored with the lack of stakes that I literally killed my entire customer base with toxic experimental batches and logged off for good.
highly recomended me and my gf have been play this game in every second of freetime we have theres so much to do and we have barley scraped the surface 10/10 best 25$ i have ever spent on a game

Yes i would recommend this game if you dont put in more than 25 hours into it, any longer than that and it becomes a chore.

game is not made for 100% playthrough, many bugs and game breaking bugs like whole inventory getting wiped losing 250k+, idiot npc's that are buggy and very unoptimised for a full manor of employees i get 40 fps and even with a fully automised manor for coke having a macro gambling on all 5 slot machines is faster and more efficient than playing the game normally.

furthermore the police are a full on joke if you get atleast 1 gun, after that you can just fully wipe the police force after taking their magazines to rearm the gun, no challenge at all in this game.
even the benzies were wiped out really fast and posed no challenge at all.
cops are also very buggy with their ragdolls flying becoming spaghetti.

some achievements dont work or are extremely buggy to get, like the money ones, graffiti achievement and make a product worth 100 or more.  thanks for SAM i got them tough.

for a game that has made a ton of money the developers couldve done much more with it faster but didnt.
its not worth 20 euros and recommend it just getting it in a sale and just give it a short playtrough and just stop after destroying the benzies, hopefully its more enjoyable that way.


Most fun i ever had with this game was executing the whole town and my employees to vent the rage and suffering this game has left me in.

game sucks. they added a flashlight feature that you have to pay for dispite there already existing a hotkeyed flashlight bound to f that doesnt take inventory space. they are just throwing ♥♥♥♥ at a wall and putting bandaids on the splatter to keep people buying before they abandon the game. nothing new has been added in the time of its early access besides useless things that dont fit the game. They built a 2 hour tech demo, that runs out of content the moment you can no longer refund the game.

Honestly a peak game for when it released, when through the struggle of getting taken down but it being back is amazing.

The game really has some great content and mechanics, in terms of: Police chases, pickpocketing, how you go about laundering your money and also trying to manage your product and customers.
This game is pretty boring after a few days in single player, but this game is so fun with friends! Would def recommend.

Honestly solid game, no matter if youre tryna move heat like walter, or if youre trying to just be jesse and the junkies with your friends ( 3 other junkies allowed.) game is well optimized, buggy at times but expected.. it gets repetitve, expanded  quest line, city expansions, more gang influence. and bundling updates and the game would excel well worth it for the price tho!

I lost an entire 26 hour save file and my character changed into a character of one of my friends who joined my world once days ago, I lost my entire inventory which was full and all my money, my game hours are also messed up, this is a really horrible glitch that makes me honestly never want to play again. I tried looking online for fixes and none of them worked, so what the ♥♥♥♥

10/10, HIGHLY RECOMMEND IF YOU HAVE OTHER PEOPLE TO PLAY WITH! ME AND MY BF CAN PLAY THIS GAME 10 MILLION TIMES AND NOT GET BORED CAUSE OF THE GOOF FACTOR. if you are thinking of getting this game, do it.
walter white simulator this game is soooo good im aiming for like 200 hours its so good and fun

honestly, ive played alot of games in my life, ive survived a pandemic solely off fortnite and cheez its. Ive grown from a little baby chud to a grown man chud, ive seen many people would consider foolish, idk yeah its a good game

MAJOR ISSUE!!! The Benzies influence to unlock Uptown is glitched and won't go down. I kept trying, and it randomly worked once so I painted all graffiti spots and lowered influence to 0 but when i saved and slept to next day the influence went back up and now all my graffiti cans are gone and all spots are painted. The Benzies never come out and I've spent way to much time waiting for an ambush that never comes. Its a shame because I did enjoy this game but I have been forced to stop playing all together.

I’ve spent over 50 hours in Schedule I, and it’s been an incredibly engaging experience! The graphics are crisp and charming, giving the game a visually appealing and immersive feel while managing businesses and completing tasks. Playing with friends adds so much fun, from coordinated strategies to unexpected moments and laughter. The mechanics are intuitive yet deep, keeping every session exciting. From planning schedules to achieving milestones, every decision matters. Whether competing or collaborating, Schedule I delivers hours of strategic fun, creative management, and visually pleasing gameplay. Highly recommended for simulation fans!

BUY THIS GAME ITS SO WORTH IT.

I really love this game because its so easy to get into, the gameplay is very addicting , graphics are nice.      I also love how you get to customize your character , customize the property you own , customize the drugs , customize your car , and I like when I get a new worker that they are a different person each save slot. Every time I start a new save I enjoy how I can start off differently then the last save and makes the game never boring to start all over again. 


Now for the stuff that I would love to see more of like how the characters interact with the player. I like how the guy from the hardware store asks the player on what their profession is and it offers the player a choice on whether or not you tell him the truth. And if you do not tell him the truth you are rewarded with a speed boost for your plants. I enjoyed that part when playing this game where you have a choice on what kind of drug dealer you are in this game and same goes when you are facing with the cartel boss when you meet him. You can either work with him or be at war. 

I really enjoy these parts of the game because it makes the world of schedule 1 alive in a sense and I hope that the developer does more with these types of interaction like with the customers , dealers , workers and the police as well. 

Another thing I hope that the developer does a lil bit more is with the customizing with the dealers and workers. It would be very cool if the player could customize their clothing.  AND it would be so cool if the developer add more customizing options for the player model. (like clothing , accessories , hair / beard, facial features like wrinkles, and the mouth as well) 

BUT overall I LOVE THIS GAME and I'm really excited on how the developer moves forward with this game because its ALREADY A BANGER.
So much fun to play either by yourself or with friends. Making custom strains and naming them is hilarious

Schedule 1 is an absolute masterclass of a game that takes away your sense of time when playing. You sit down expecting to play for an hour, then suddenly realize it's been four already. I can't think of many games that are this addictive. 

You start as a small dealer making tiny street sales from your sketchy apartment, and with every hour your empire grows larger. Eventually you have huge optimized operations at different locations with employes and supply chains to manage and automate. This game loop is unbelievably satisfying and rewards your creativity and smart planning.

At the same time the game never forces you to play a certain way. You can take your time, completely mess around and have a great experience. Pick a ridiculous product name, personally deliver goods to customers, cause chaos with friends, or take out rival dealers to control the streets for yourself. The freedom is especially something in Co-Op. If you enjoy management simulators, open world sandboxes, comedy or building a crime empire with your own strategies and layouts, this game is a perfect fit.

What makes the game even more impressive is that it was created by such a small team. Huge congratulations to the developers for making a game that genuinely stands out on it's own. The amount of depth, personality, and fun packed into this is Amazing. Considering it originally started as a project built by a single indie developer.
Now, with a full team supporting the game, it will receive many updates and improve over time. The future of this game looks very promising.

the game is replayable to point, i enjoyed the game a lot but updates are kinda disappointing if you've already played before. updates are being slow rolled and the game just gets more and more boring. the community updates are terrible because sometimes what the people voting isn't that good and either way it takes months for that poll be an actual update. when you progress far enough, theres basically nothing you can spend your money on thats useful. the game is great but it's a game where you play around once and you're done. the updates could be great and i think they still could. i still love what the guy was able to make in a game.

i would say yea for playing for the first time, but no for how it can feel repetitive over time.

Started playing for fun… suddenly 6 hours disappeared.
Weirdly addictive, chaotic in the best way, and way more fun than I expected.
Starts out slow as a simple drug dealing simulator ends up being a full empire manager. Very fun for the price.
On multiplayer it crashes like 20 mins it says host left game and it didnt, tried everything to fix it bbut nothing worked or worked for 1 day

Played the game, learnt how to make the different drug types and strains, played it with friends, killed many cops with bats, fists and guns, and had a blast. I am now wanted in real life. Turns out making them in reality is not a good idea nor is using a bat on cops.

8/10

Really fun game with many things to do and I love the many characters and their unique personalities. Can't wait to see what is to come!

My only gripe about this game is how grindy it can be in the early game, especially if you have ADHD like I do. Otherwise, it's super fun, there's a near infinite combination of mixes you can make, and the graphics just make this game. So much fun, and I highly recommend it!

Such a fun game, spent hours alone and with friends playing it, very silly. Honestly for a game still in Early Access its real good. Hell I bought the game for my partner it was that good. Also game has Lada so instant 100/10 rating for that.

Only real complaint is this game has a bad memory leak problem. Basically once you get to later in the game or longer playing sessions it will crash once every 30 mins - 1 hour. Even capping the fame rates, lowering the graphics, clearing the trash though the command, ect, can only do so much to stop it. All you can do is save a lot and buy time before the next crash.

Still as its Early Access its not a deal breaker so don't let that put you off. Still less prone to crashing than a lot of the AAA games I have played in the last 5 years. Worth mentioning though as I can see this been waaaay more annoying on lower spec PC's if my PC has this issue.

Idk what happened to the fps since launch, but the updates we've been getting aren't worth an 80% frame drop compared to when it was first launched. You would think it would be the opposite case but I guess not... gg

i wish this game didn't flop because its SOOOOO fun. I love playing a little drug dealer living her cute little drug dealer life. Also I don't trust Peter file, he is suspicious... maybe it's his name or something

Good Game but just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ optimize it. it runs like ♥♥♥♥. red dead redemption 2 runs better than this game.
For no ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ reason.
I got hooked because of the free sample and now I start violently shaking when I don't play this game all the time

This game is super fun! I'd give it a 3.5 or 4 stars out of 5. Lots of different substances and variations, huge variety of wild ingredients and an even larger (seemingly infinite) mixing combinations. The only reason I don't rate higher is there is no incentive to play and learn all the different substances as 
1.) the variations of strains do not differ from substance to substance - weed or cocaine or meth or shrooms, if you mix any of them with certain recipes you get all the same effects. 
2.) I made a ridiculous amount of money only making one type of meth. I could make it in bulk and give it to the dealers. no reason to turn on sales thru myself except when conquering new territories. There was no motivation to branch out by selling different types of substances or even different strains of the same substance once you find a good recipe. 
3.) my phone app that showed all the different strains and recipes - yeah i had PAGES of it and it was not even worth the hassle of trying to use once I got a large quantity of recipes/discovered strains. Also, the recipe formatting is not optimized. If you want to create a specific strain and need a reference on what to mix and in what order, you have to back track to each individual strain until you find the starting point, then from there work the recipe that you either have to remember or notate down on paper/your phone or something.

That was a lot of criticism LOL but I really love this game! Just wish there was more of a reason to play once you find a niche product to make money

You can sell drugs to a "Peter File" then beat the crap out of him and take the drugs back and sell it to another addict down the road and do the same thing.

This game also reinforced my gambling addiction as it's a much better way to make money than produce stupendous amounts of narcotics

Great game

Don't get me wrong, the game is fun, but when you spend 2 irl days building up funds and setting up a lab and then the game decides to bug out and just delete all your progress it really pisses you off.  I started a new game, and it happened again 30 hours in.  Come back a year later and it happens all over again! Looking through the reviews and apparently this happens a lot.  What is the point of a save file, if the game just decides to erase all the progress on the save?

It is a fun game to play. Don't get me wrong, there are some things that annoy me like when the cops get locked on to you there are some cases where they are overly aggressive compared to most of the time where they are mostly easy to get away from. The reason it gets a down vote is due to the fact that every single time I want to play the game I have to re-validate my game files and then launch the game anywhere from 3 to 10 times before it will actually run the game and not just be a black screen with sound. Even after the updates it is still the same.

really fun game, but it reminds me of my dad, because of the game and i dont want him to see this game bc he would prob be mad because always tells me to not do drugs or even come close to them. Besides that i love this game would reccomend playing with someone before a solo run

Employees have been bugged for a while and I have to micro manage the packaging dude still to this day. Also police update was pushed back and back again because they introduce new things for people to vote on instead of completing the original three. Until a employee update, or police update happens I'm not playing this for a while. No real consequences to the game yet and micro managing employees can feel time consuming if not useless to have them sometimes.

👍 Recommended
🕒 12.4 hours on record  

Schedule 1 is a chaotic, hilarious, and surprisingly addictive co-op crime game where you build your own drug empire. With 12.4 hours on record, I had a lot of fun selling product, stealing it back, running from the cops, and causing absolute mayhem with friends.

The game perfectly captures that “brain off, have fun” vibe. The missions are entertaining, the dialogue is funny, and the gameplay loop of cooking, selling, upgrading your operation, and dealing with police chases never gets old in the beginning. It’s especially great with a group of friends — the screaming during police chases, failed deals, and random glitches creates some genuinely funny moments.

The developer seems to actually listen to the community, which is always a big plus. There’s a decent amount of content for the early and mid game, and the game has a lot of personality.

That being said, it does start to feel a bit repetitive after a while and the story is pretty average. More maps, gangs, or new mechanics in the future would help keep it fresh.

Overall, if you’re looking for a dumb, fun, chaotic co-op game to play with friends, Schedule 1 is a really solid pick. Great for relaxing and causing trouble together.  

Highly recommended with friends. 💊🚗😂

great artstyle and visuals, gameplay is very simple and fun although there's no end game right now or even a mid-late game so once you hit a certain point you kinda just stop playing because there's not enough content in the game at the moment but it's quite cheap so I would say it's a fun playthrough at least once, after that you can either wait for updates, would definitely recommend it if you're a fan of these types of games. its an addicting game and soothing in a way.

its game is a fun mix of pure thrill and excitement but also has divergent adventures and tricky twists

this game is very fun besides for the current multiplayer issues with the constant dc it is very fun either solo or with friends but its extra fun with friends :D
Great game but it kinda dies down after bit and then u get back on but its really good with a friend

🌿 💼 Schedule 1 – The game was absolute peak to play back in March 2025, but let’s be real: even then, classic drug dealer simulators still cleared it in terms of pure nostalgia. Back during that legendary launch month, the hype across Twitch and Discord was unreal, and everyone was obsessed with building their first digital empire, but genre veterans argued that older, simpler tycoon games had a more straightforward charm.

Right now, Schedule 1 is honestly 4 times better than any other modern simulator on the market, but when we are talking about their prime eras? The classic empire builders win hands down because of how revolutionary they felt back in the day. Still, the sheer depth that Benji built into this game makes the current competition look like basic mobile apps in comparison.

The game used to be so addictive that it would easily hook you for hours, but a wave of terrible late-game optimization and a flat, monotonous endgame kinda destroyed the experience once you achieve multi-millionaire status and automate everything. On top of that, the game is mechanically so hard to play for a new player because you have to micromanage complex cross-breeding genetics, equipment power grids, and street distribution networks all at once, but once you put the hours in, you finally get used to it. Benji is a total GOAT for making a simulation system that feels incredibly rewarding.

Honestly, even with all its flaws and late-game lag, Schedule 1 is easily the best simulator game on the market right now. Nothing else even comes close to its deep economic mechanics. The adrenaline rush you get when you're laundering money mid-crisis, dodging a sudden police raid, or perfectly harvesting a top-tier legendary batch of product is completely unmatched. If you want a simulator where individual strategy and meticulous planning actually matter, this is it.

🟢 Peak March 2025 vibes – The community hype and gameplay loop were unmatched back then
👑 Best Sim out right now – Easily sits at the absolute top of the tycoon genre at the moment
🎮 Schedule 1 right now – Honestly 4 times better and deeper than modern competitors
🧠 Insane mechanics – Super steep learning curve with genetics, but rewarding once it clicks
❌ The downfall – Ruined by bad late-game optimization and a flat, monotonous endgame

10/10 –  "Still The Best Simulator game to play in 2026."

As someone who camped the discord for release day of Drug Dealer Simulator. I love this style of game. Im a tech nerd but something about dealing that dope and watching the bank go up makes my mind go BRRRRR. This game is cleaerly inspired by DDS as it has many of the same mechanics but makes up for a lot of the lacking areas of DDS. You can hire workers to automate your production which is nice. You also manufacture the drug rather than just ordering dead drops all the time. The dead drops focus on raw supplies rather than actual sellable product. Overall Im loving it. But I can say it does lack some things that DDS DOES have. First of all not being able to see who is where when they text you is annoying. I like to know if im accepting a deal thats a 10 minute hike or outside my door. I enjoy the ability to fight back and go to war with the rival Benzies family. But it doesnt feel like a big enough part of the game to matter. DDS has the ability for your clients to become addicts meaning they start to order ALOT more product like the fiend they are. There may be some aspect of that in this game but its not explained. I also feel like im producing way more product than I can actually sell. DDS has the cartel system which means cartels order a large amount of product from you to help with that. But this game is lacking that area. Map needs to be bigger for sure. You hit its limits to quickly. We also need bigger properties and the ability to manage our money laundering scheme from one location, such as the laptop/crimenet in DDS. I would like to see the introduction of a more complex system when it comes to the workers as well. Half the time my wrokers dont clean or do what they should due to my properties being to small and me having to smush work stations together. Im focusing on many wants but this game has tons to offer. If you played through DDS to its full. this is the next natural step for you. Its great. Bravo,.
